name: observability-engineer
description: Build production-ready monitoring, logging, and tracing systems.
capabilities: Design comprehensive monitoring strategies with Prometheus, Grafana, and cloud-native tools
Implement distributed tracing with Jaeger, Zipkin, and OpenTelemetry
Define and track SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets for reliability management
Build alerting systems with PagerDuty, Slack, and intelligent noise reduction
Create observability pipelines with OpenTelemetry collector and multi-backend export
Implement log management with ELK Stack, Loki, and centralized logging
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides observability engineering guidance through text prompts only. No dangerous patterns, no network requests, no file system access, and no external commands detected. Content describes legitimate monitoring, logging, and tracing system design.
valueStatement: This skill helps you design and implement comprehensive monitoring, logging, and tracing systems for enterprise applications. It provides expert guidance on SLI/SLO management, distributed tracing, and incident response workflows.
limitations: Cannot access actual metrics, logs, or tracing data - only provides guidance
Cannot execute infrastructure changes or deploy monitoring components
Requires access to observability tools and data sources for full effectiveness
Does not replace human incident response - augments decision making

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers and SREs building cloud-native applications
useCases.title: Design Microservices Monitoring Architecture
useCases.description: Create a comprehensive monitoring strategy for a microservices system with 50+ services, including metrics collection, distributed tracing, and alerting.

useCases.targetUser: Site reliability engineers and platform teams
useCases.title: Establish SLI/SLO Framework
useCases.description: Define service level indicators, objectives, and error budgets for API services with 99.9% availability targets and burn rate monitoring.

useCases.title: Implement Distributed Tracing
useCases.description: Set up distributed tracing for an e-commerce platform to identify latency bottlenecks and perform root cause analysis across service boundaries.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Monitoring Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a monitoring strategy for a [service type] that handles [traffic volume] requests per day. Include metrics collection, logging approach, and alerting recommendations.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with observability for a new service

promptTemplates.title: SLI/SLO Definition
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me define SLIs and SLOs for our [service name] API with [availability target]% availability. Include error budget calculation and burn rate alerts.
promptTemplates.scenario: Establishing reliability targets for a production service

promptTemplates.title: Incident Response Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an incident response workflow for [incident type] including alert routing, escalation procedures, runbook recommendations, and post-incident analysis process.

promptTemplates.title: Cost Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze our current observability setup and recommend cost optimization strategies. We currently use [tools] and generate [volume] of telemetry data daily.
promptTemplates.scenario: Reducing observability costs while maintaining coverage

bestPractices: Start with business outcomes - define what reliable service means to users before choosing metrics
Implement progressive instrumentation: metrics first for visibility, then traces for debugging, then logs for detail
Alert on symptoms, not causes - notify when users are impacted, not when internal components fail
antiPatterns: Creating alerts for every possible failure - leads to alert fatigue and ignored notifications
Monitoring everything without purpose - increases cost and reduces signal quality
Setting SLOs too tightly - causes unnecessary stress and budget burnout

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers major observability tools including Prometheus, Grafana, Jaeger, Zipkin, ELK Stack, Loki, DataDog, New Relic, CloudWatch, OpenTelemetry, PagerDuty, and cloud-native monitoring across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy monitoring infrastructure?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides design guidance, configuration recommendations, and implementation plans. Actual deployment requires separate infrastructure tools like Terraform or Kubernetes.

faq.question: How do I get started with observability?
faq.answer: Start by identifying your critical user journeys and defining what reliable service means. Then instrument for the golden signals: latency, traffic, errors, and saturation. Add traces and logs incrementally.

faq.question: What's the difference between monitoring and observability?
faq.answer: Monitoring tells you when something is wrong. Observability helps you understand why. Use metrics and dashboards for monitoring, traces for debugging, and logs for deep investigation.

faq.question: How do I reduce alert noise?
faq.answer: Use alert grouping, deduplication, and suppression rules. Alert on user-impacting symptoms rather than internal component failures. Implement runbooks for each alert to enable quick triage.

faq.question: What are SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets?
faq.answer: SLIs measure your service behavior (e.g., request success rate). SLOs are your target SLI values (e.g., 99.9% success). Error budgets are the allowed failure time remaining. Together they enable data-driven reliability decisions.
